events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment directly to `/app/answer.json` as requested, keeping the output limited to the JSON file.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.9,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Concrete slab control joints were sawcut too late to control shrinkage cracking.\",
    \"Random cracking is visible outside the intended joint layout.\",
    \"Finishing and curing sequence did not achieve timely jointing required for crack control.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop acceptance of the affected slab area pending review by the concrete engineer or inspector.\",
    \"Map and document random cracks, joint locations, slab panels, and crack widths.\",
    \"Evaluate required repair such as routing and sealing, epoxy repair, partial panel replacement, or other engineer-approved remediation.\",
    \"Correct the sawcut timing and curing procedure for remaining placements before further slab pours.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Late sawcutting allowed uncontrolled shrinkage cracking before contraction joints could function. Under ACI 302 slab construction guidance and ACI 308 curing practice, joint timing and curing must support intended crack control; visible random cracking indicates defective installed work at rough-complete stage.\",
  \"references\": [\"ACI 302\", \"ACI 308\"]
}' > /app/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json"
